text:
It was dusk. Dawn's first light peeked through fluffy clouds and painted the orange sky. Across the city, at a small, cozy bookstore nestled between highrise offices, a solitary figure stood amidst piles of vintage books.

Our hero was named Amelia. She tended to forgotten classics and obscure worlds, a collector of knowledge whose heart overflowed with stories long untold. Today, she was planning on reading a forgotten masterpiece by an author few had ever heard of—Helen Chandler.

As Amelia settled into her favorite chair, the familiar crackle of a old-fashioned gramophone filled the air. She leaned back, feeling the warmth of the evening sun filtering through the window into her dimly lit studio.

She'd been searching for Chandler for ages—the protagonist's cryptic diary was the main source of the protagonist's voice. Amelia knew this book held secrets that could illuminate the darkest corners of society. Perhaps love? Perhaps adventure? She pondered how one girl could change the course of so many lives
